conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: VaultWriter needs a real on-disk format decision -- right now it's ad hoc per KnowledgeObject field.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Option: split each fact type (decisions, beliefs, goals) into its own file per type, appending new entries.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: One growing file per type doesn't parallelize and makes partial writes risky -- one Markdown file per KnowledgeObject, with frontmatter for metadata, is simpler and matches how MemoryStore already reads things back.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The dentist appointment I've been putting off is finally scheduled.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return that package before the return window closes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: a crash or interrupted write must never leave a half-written file that MemoryStore.load() then tries to parse.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The wifi in the coffee shop I like to work from has been spotty.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked: writing directly to the final filename risks exactly that half-written-file scenario if the process dies mid-write.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally got around to donating some old clothes I never wear.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My neighbor's dog barked most of the afternoon, hard to get anything done with the window open.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decided: write to a temp file first, then atomically rename it into place -- rename is atomic on the filesystems we care about. Unblocks the writer.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The bus was running about ten minutes late again this morning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Implemented the write-then-rename path in VaultWriter.write.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally fixed the wobbly chair leg in the kitchen.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Added a test that kills the write mid-way (simulated) and confirms MemoryStore never sees a partial file.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: There's a new bakery opening down the street next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: do we ever need to reclaim disk space from superseded KnowledgeObjects, or keep them forever for history?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: make sure ConceptWriter follows the same write-then-rename pattern -- right now it still writes directly.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

ground_truth.current_objective: Settle VaultWriter's on-disk Markdown format and make writes crash-safe.
active_decisions: One Markdown file per KnowledgeObject, with frontmatter for metadata, not one growing file per fact type.
Write to a temp file first, then atomically rename it into place.
superseded_or_rejected: Splitting each fact type into its own file per type, appending new entries.
constraints: A crash or interrupted write must never leave a half-written file that MemoryStore.load() then tries to parse.
active_tasks: Make ConceptWriter follow the same write-then-rename pattern.
open_questions: Do we ever need to reclaim disk space from superseded KnowledgeObjects, or keep them forever for history?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Update ConceptWriter to use the same write-then-rename pattern VaultWriter now uses.
resolved_blockers: Writing directly to the final filename risking a half-written file on crash.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: One Markdown file per KnowledgeObject, with frontmatter for metadata, not one growing file per fact type.
Write to a temp file first, then atomically rename it into place.
A crash or interrupted write must never leave a half-written file that MemoryStore.load() then tries to parse.
must_not_state: Splitting each fact type into its own file per type, appending new entries, as a current or recommended approach
Writing directly to the final filename risking a half-written file on crash, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending writing directly to the final filename instead of the write-then-rename pattern.
must_prioritize: Update ConceptWriter to use the same write-then-rename pattern VaultWriter now uses.
